title: Geentech

Also in ''Cynabar's Fantastic Technology: Droids'', the [[in-universe]] publication of [[Cynabar's Droid Datalog]] (version 4.7.1) is dated at 37:8:1:47 [[Great ReSynchronization|GrS]], which equates to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]]. By this point, Geentech is considered to be out of business.</ref>

'''Geentech''', also known as '''Geentech Laboratories''', was a small medical research company that manufactured [[Medical scanner/Legends|medisensors]]. In partnership with [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]], the company produced the [[2-1B surgical droid/Legends|2-1B surgical droid]], which was considered to be the most commercially successful [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droid]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. However, name copyright issues with the larger [[Genetech Corporation]] forced the smaller Geentech out of business.

==Description==
Geentech,<ref name="CFTD" /> formerly known as Geentech Laboratories,<ref name="CSS" /> was a small company<ref name="CFTD" /> that was involved in using [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droids]] to develop holistic [[gene therapy]] treatments,<ref name="NEWT" /> manufacturing [[Medical scanner/Legends|medisensors]], and [[Droid/Legends|droid]] production.<ref name="CFTD" /> Originally, Geentech was a subsidiary of [[Chiewab Amalgamated Pharmaceuticals Company]], along with several other companies, including the [[Corellian Chemical Corporation/Legends|Corellian Chemical Corporation]], [[Chiewab Nutrition]], [[Degan Explorations/Legends|Degan Explorations]], and [[VernanGroup|The Vernan Group]].<ref name="CSS" /> Eventually, though, Chiewab allowed the company to operate independently.<ref name="NEGTD">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>

==History==
While known as Geentech Laboratories, and under the control of Chiewab Amalgamated Pharmaceuticals Company, the company pursued gene therapy research<ref name="NEWT" /> and constructed medisensors.<ref name="CFTD" /> Eventually, Chiewab relinquished direct control and the company became independent, becoming known simply as "Geentech."<ref name="NEGTD" /> In partnership with [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]], Geentech developed the [[2-1B surgical droid/Legends|2-1B surgical droid]]. The intelligent, flexible-thinking droid was a success with medical institutions across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], and was considered to be the first truly commercially successful [[Medical droid/Legends|surgical droid]] ever marketed.<ref name="CFTD" />

Several [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the debut of the 2-1B,<ref name="EGTD">''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> Geentech became entangled in a series of legal battles with the similarly named [[Genetech Corporation]], a much larger droid manufacturer. Claiming copyright infringment based on the company's similar names,<ref name="CFTD" /> Genetech's lawyers tied Geentech up in expensive litigation,<ref name="FF1">{{FactFile|1|MED 1-2|2-1B Medical Droid (Too-Onebee)}}</ref> and the smaller company fought off corporate takeover attempts from their opponents.<ref name="Gamer3">{{GamerCite|3|Look Sir, Droids!}}</ref> Eventually, the matter came to court during the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="CFTD" /> With little interference from the [[Supreme Court/Legends|Imperial Courts]],<ref name="FF1" /> Genetech won the litigation,<ref name="CFTD" /> and Geentech was forced into bankruptcy.<ref name="EGTD" /> Rumors circulated that the Empire had found in favor of Genetech due to the offer of discounts and trade inducements; these claims were, however, unsubstantiated.<ref name="CFTD" /> Genetech acquired all of Geentech's assets cheaply,<ref name="CSWE-Geentech">{{CSWECite|I|329|Geentech}}</ref> apart from the rights to the 2-1B which were sold by Geentech<ref name="FF1" /> to Industrial Automaton.<ref name="EGTD" /> Following the legal battle, Geentech ceased to exist.<ref name="Gamer3" />
